codeReview.summary: Feature consolidation is complete and verified. Two workers collaborated: session 3ef357ea created the canonical shared-helpers modules (discovery-shared-helpers.js for browser, scripts/discovery-shared-helpers.mjs for Node.js), updated all four wizard scripts to delegate with fallback, and updated verify-discovery-webhook.mjs to import from the shared module. Session 13835792 verified the work and confirmed all tests pass. The implementation addresses all five expected behavior items from the feature description.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The COPIES comment in discovery-shared-helpers.js documents that 6 copies of these functions must be manually kept in sync. This is a documented convention, but it's fragile - no build step enforces consistency. This is a known maintenance burden documented in the code itself.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: discovery-shared-helpers.js lines 12-20 list: discovery-wizard-local.js, discovery-wizard-probes.js, discovery-wizard-relay.js, discovery-wizard-verify.js, scripts/verify-discovery-webhook.mjs, app.js

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill procedure specifies to use 'curl' for HTTP/service assertions and 'agent-browser' for browser-visible paths. For worker-only discovery behavior with no meaningful browser signal, the skill allows 'seeded sheet readback or other deterministic contract evidence' as substitution. The worker followed procedure: ran targeted contract tests and repo validation, plus curl/HTTP checks where appropriate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification shows npm run test:contract:all, npm run test:browser-use-discovery, and node --check for all wizard scripts. The worker correctly identified this as worker-only behavior without requiring browser automation.

summary: PASS - Feature consolidation is complete. Two workers collaborated: 3ef357ea created the canonical shared-helpers modules (browser + Node.js) and updated all wizard scripts to delegate with fallback; 13835792 verified the work. All 69 browser-use-discovery tests, 40 repo validation tests, and all contract suites pass. The SQLite ExperimentalWarning is documented as cosmetic (pre-existing, not caused by this feature). The implementation fully covers the feature's expected behavior: helper duplication reduced, contract drift halted, run-status persistence documented, future workers have one canonical path, and local-vs-hosted sheetId assumptions are correctly handled.
